questions.stem: A company has three departments (Sales, Support, HR) each building their own AI agents with different teams. They want centralized billing but isolated agent data between departments. What is the recommended Agent Platform structure?

options.text: One workspace with three projects, using project roles to control access
Three separate workspaces with no organizational link
One project containing all agents from all three departments

questions.explanation: One workspace with three projects provides centralized billing and team management while keeping agent data isolated between departments. Each project has its own agents, knowledge bases, sessions, and environment variables. Project roles (Admin, Editor, Viewer) control who can access each department's resources. If they needed separate billing, an Organization grouping multiple workspaces would be the alternative.

questions.stem: An IT administrator needs to ensure that all workspace members authenticate through Okta before accessing the Agent Platform. What must they configure, and on which plan?

options.text: SAML 2.0 SSO with domain verification and Force SSO enabled, available on Professional plans
SAML 2.0 SSO with domain verification and Force SSO enabled, available on Enterprise plans only
Multi-factor authentication enforcement, available on all plans

questions.explanation: SSO (both SAML 2.0 and OIDC) is an Enterprise plan feature. To mandate Okta authentication, the admin configures SAML SSO, verifies the company's email domain via DNS TXT record, and enables Force SSO. Once forced, all members with verified domain emails must authenticate through Okta. The workspace Owner retains password-based login as a fallback.

questions.stem: In Agent Platform's workspace role hierarchy, the ___ role can manage agent deployments and monitor production but cannot invite team members or configure AI models.
questions.explanation: The Operator role sits between Member and Admin in the hierarchy. Operators can deploy agents, view analytics, manage environment variables, and view sessions. They cannot invite or remove team members, configure LLM models, or manage secrets -- those are Admin-level permissions.

questions.stem: A workspace Admin sends an invitation to a new team member. The recipient does not act on it for 10 days. What happens?

options.text: The invitation remains active indefinitely until the recipient acts
The invitation expires after 7 days and is automatically cleaned up; the Admin can resend a new one
The recipient's account is automatically created in a pending state after 7 days

questions.explanation: Workspace invitations expire automatically after 7 days. When an invitation expires, it is cleaned up automatically. The Admin can resend a fresh invitation from the pending invitations list, which generates a new token and resets the expiry. No account is created until the recipient accepts.

questions.stem: A company is concerned about data leakage between their workspace and a competitor who also uses Agent Platform. Which platform design principle directly addresses this concern?

options.text: Role-based access control (RBAC) within each workspace
Tenant isolation enforced at the data layer, where every database query includes a tenant filter and cross-tenant access returns 'not found' rather than 'access denied'
Encryption of all data at rest using AES-256

questions.explanation: Tenant isolation is enforced at the database layer, not the application layer. Every data query includes the tenant identifier as a filter condition, so there is no API path that can access another tenant's data. Cross-tenant attempts return 'not found' to prevent even confirming whether a resource exists. RBAC controls access within a tenant, and encryption protects data at rest, but neither addresses cross-tenant data isolation specifically.
